CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, str_value VARCHAR(100));
SELECT right(str_value, 4) AS result FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT right(str_value, 2) AS result FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2;
SELECT right(str_value, 10) AS result FROM test_strings WHERE id = 3;
SELECT right(str_value, 0) AS result FROM test_strings WHERE id = 4;
SELECT right(str_value, 1) AS result FROM test_strings WHERE id = 5;
SELECT right((SELECT str_value FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1), 4) AS subquery_result;
SELECT t1.id, right(t2.str_value, 4) AS rightmost FROM test_strings t1 JOIN test_strings t2 ON t1.id != t2.id;
SELECT id, right(str_value, CASE WHEN LENGTH(str_value) < 5 THEN LENGTH(str_value) ELSE 5 END) AS custom_result FROM test_strings;
SELECT right((SELECT MIN(str_value) FROM test_strings), 3) AS min_rightmost;
SELECT id, right(str_value, COUNT(*)) AS variable_count FROM test_strings GROUP BY id;
